Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 7 points with 7 lead changes and 27 ties. The game saw 9 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 63%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q3, 7:00, Porziņģis (BOS): Porzingis 15' Jump Shot (12 PTS) (Brown 5 AST). It swung the win probability by +14.8 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q3, 9:22, White (BOS): White 3PT Jump Shot (10 PTS) (Tatum 9 AST). It swung the win probability by -14.4 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 8, away team 11.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q2, 2:16: Doncic 26' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (23 PTS). That single play moved BOS's chance of winning from 65% to 48%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went DAL's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Dončić of DAL. Across 58 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 106 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 10:48, with BOS down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 43%. In other words: very realistic, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q2, 0:37.1, with the game tilting BOS's way. The play in the middle of it: Holiday 3PT Jump Shot (17 PTS) (Tatum 8 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
